THIS CASE REMAINS IN ITS CURRENT GROUPING, AND THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE IS MAINTAINED. ORDER SIGNED AND FILED ON 5-14-08 IN CASE #444221 LUJAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS. JUDGE ROBERTSON; CLERK: C. LI; REPORTER: J. LONG (220)

EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANYS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARYADJUDICATION - GREGORY TYLER SFSC 451213 (TRANSACTION ID # 21496584) FILED BY DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHIFTED THE BURDEN IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AND INTERROGATORY RESPONSES CONCERNING USE OF DEFENDANT'S ASBESTOS GLOVES. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, BUT OTHERWISE DENIED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
TODD THACKER, ESQ. (JACKSON & WALLACE, LLP), (415)982-6300, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY
JEFFREY WEHR, ESQ. (BRAYTON PURCELL, LLP), (415)898-1555, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, GREGORY TYLER

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHIFTED THE BURDEN IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AND INTERROGATORY RESPONSES CONCERNING USE OF DEFENDANT'S ASBESTOS GLOVES. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, BUT OTHERWISE DENIED. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
